title: Buttondown vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Buttondown vs Sendinblue: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between Buttondown and Sendinblue comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Buttondown is built for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while Sendinblue targets transactional email and SMS campaigns. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Developers And Technical Writers Who Prefer Markdown

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on developers and technical writers who prefer markdown
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on transactional email and SMS campaigns

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Buttondown wins for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while Sendinblue is stronger for transactional email and SMS campaigns. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Buttondown starts at a lower price point ($9/mo vs $25/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Sendinblue to Buttondown is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
